title: Introducing Attention Weights
concept: Attention calculates a weight for each input word at each step, showing how much context to pull from that specific word.

options.text: How much focus to place on each input word
How fast the model should run
The total word count of the document

exercises.explain: Attention weights tell the model how much focus (or context) to draw from each word in the input sentence.

cards.title: The AI Spotlight
cards.body: Have you ever wondered how AI understands which 'bank' you mean when you talk about a river bank versus a money bank? It doesn't guess randomly—it uses a system of mathematical spotlights called attention weights to focus on the surrounding words!

cards.title: What are Attention Weights?
cards.body: Attention weights are numbers between 0 and 1 assigned to each input word. They act like a spotlight, showing how much focus or 'weight' a model should place on a specific word when generating the next part of a sentence. Higher weights mean more focus!

cards.title: The Spice Rack Dial
cards.body: Imagine you are cooking and trying to add the perfect blend of spices. When adding heat, you focus heavily on the chili powder (high weight) while giving very little thought to the vanilla extract (low weight). Attention weights act as dial knobs, turning up the volume on the most relevant ingredients for your current step while turning down the rest.

cards.title: Contextual Clues in Action
cards.body: In the sentence "The bank of the river was muddy," if the AI is translating the word "bank," the attention weights will be very high for "river" and "muddy." This high weight tells the AI to choose the water-related meaning of "bank" rather than a financial one.

cards.title: Myth: All Words are Equal
cards.body: Many people think that AI treats all words in a sentence with equal importance. In reality, attention weights allow the model to completely ignore filler words like "the" or "and" when it needs to focus on key action words and nouns.

cards.title: Shifting the Spotlight
cards.body: Let's compare how attention weights shift focus depending on what the model is trying to understand.

left.text: In 'bank vault', the word 'vault' gets the highest attention weight.

right.text: In 'bank water', the word 'water' gets the highest attention weight.

exercises.prompt: What does a high attention weight (close to 1) tell us about a word?

options.text: The word is highly relevant to the current step.
The word is the very first word in the dictionary.
The word is a common typo.

exercises.explain: Attention weights range from 0 to 1, where higher numbers represent a stronger spotlight or focus on that specific word.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Attention weights remain constant and never change as the AI generates a sentence.
exercises.explain: Attention weights are highly dynamic. They change at each step of the process to focus on whatever words are most relevant to the current output.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to describe how AI measures focus.
exercises.sentence: The AI uses attention ________ to decide how much focus to give each word.
exercises.explain: Attention weights act as numerical values that tell the model which words deserve the most focus when processing information.

exercises.prompt: Identify which AI system has an incorrect explanation of attention weights:

options.text: AI system A: 'Attention weights are values between 0 and 1 that highlight context.'
AI system B: 'Attention weights help resolve which meaning of a word is being used.'
AI system C: 'Attention weights are fixed settings that never change during a sentence.'

exercises.explain: The third choice contains an incorrect statement; attention weights are not fixed and must change as the sentence generates to shift focus to new words.

exercises.prompt: When a model is generating a long paragraph, how do the attention weights behave at each new step?

options.text: They stay exactly the same for every word generated.
They recalculate dynamically at each step.
They are randomly generated and never change.
They only look at the very first word of the sentence.

exercises.explain: Attention weights dynamically adjust at every step of generating a sentence, allowing the model to look at different words as the context demands. They are not fixed once the sentence starts.
